# Healing Soil — Handmade Soap from Goa

Healing Soil is a small-batch soap maker in Goa, India. We craft handmade soaps using glycerin, goat milk, and shea butter bases. All our soaps are made to order, SLS-free, and shipped across India.

## Brand basics

- **Founded:** 2025
- **Location:** Goa, India
- **Products:** Handmade natural soaps for sensitive skin
- **Shipping:** India only, 4-day dispatch + 3–7 days transit
- **Contact:** WhatsApp for orders (see website)

## Product categories

### Glycerin soap base
- Light, clear bars for oily or combination skin
- Glycerin stays in the bar (commercial soap removes it because glycerin sells separately)
- Gentle cleansing without heavy feel
- Everyday bar for warm months

**Examples:** Honey Oats Glycerin, Turmeric Glycerin

### Goat milk soap base
- Creamy, versatile for sensitive, dry, or eczema-prone skin
- Contains lactic acid for gentle exfoliation, vitamins A–E, pH-balanced
- Most popular for first-time buyers switching from commercial soap
- Nourishing without being heavy

**Examples:** Neem Tulsi Goat Milk, Kesar Haldi Goat Milk

### Shea butter soap base
- Rich, deeply moisturising for very dry, mature, winter, or tight-feeling skin
- Portion of shea butter survives soap-making, stays deposited on skin
- Anti-inflammatory, does not clog pores
- Too rich for oily or acne-prone skin

**Examples:** Red Wine Shea Butter (coming soon)

## Key ingredient facts

- **SLS-free:** All our soaps are made without sodium lauryl sulfate (detergent common in commercial soap)
- **Bases:** Glycerin, goat milk, shea butter sourced from a manufacturer used since 2025; neem and tulsi grown on the farm
- **Fragrance:** Plant-based or unscented; no synthetic fragrance
- **Made to order:** Each batch made fresh; no two are identical; we replace damaged bars in transit

- **Reviews:** https://healingsoil.in (embedded in homepage)

## How handmade soap differs from commercial soap

**Why commercial soap removes glycerin:** Glycerin is a natural byproduct of soap-making. Commercial manufacturers remove it because glycerin sells for more as a separate product (face creams, lotions). Healing Soil keeps it in.

**Why we avoid SLS:** Sodium lauryl sulfate is a synthetic detergent that creates thick lather in commercial bars. It is also a common cause of skin reactions, especially for sensitive skin. Our soaps lather lightly because there is no SLS and because they contain glycerin or goat milk, which do not produce the same foam.

**Why made-to-order matters:** Each bar is made fresh with ingredients from the farm (neem, tulsi). No batch is identical, and we do not pretend otherwise. Natural variation is part of handmade. What we promise: real ingredients, transparent process, damage-free arrival (we replace damaged bars).

## FAQs

**Do you ship outside India?**
Not currently. India only.

**How long does a bar last?**
Most bars last 4–6 weeks with daily use, depending on how you store and dry them between washes.

**Can I return a bar?**
We do not accept returns. If your bar arrives damaged, we replace it. If the soap does not work for your skin, that learning is valuable and costs less than a dermatologist visit.

**How should I store my soap?**
Keep it dry between washes. A perforated soap dish or hanging pouch works well. In humid months, rotate bars so each one dries fully between uses.

**Is handmade soap safe for eczema or psoriasis?**
Handmade soap does not treat skin conditions. But it will not make them worse, which is more than most commercial soaps can say. Many customers with eczema or sensitive skin report that goat milk or shea butter soap feels better than anything else they have tried. Patch-test on a small area first if you are very reactive.
